_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Progressive motor weakness of more than one limb and partial or total areflexia
Involvement of ventilatory muscles – respiratory distress / shallow respiration, weak voice, inability to cough effectively and decreased inability to count in single breath.

Progression of symptoms upto 4 weeks, relative symmetry, mild sensory sign or symptoms, facial nerve (50%) or other cranial nerve involvement, transient or absent bladder involvement, recovery 2-4 weeks after nadir, autonomous dysfunction and absence of fever at the onset.

clinical_thresholds.note: CSF report must suggest elevated protein
evidenced by prolonged F-wave, distal latencies, decreased conduction velocities, conduction block

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment
Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) analysis and Nerve Conduction study (NCS) report

claim.label: Indoor case papers with treatment given details

ppd_preauth.q: Was the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Detailed Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) analysis and Nerve Conduction study (NCS) report submitted?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor Case Papers with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Was the Electrophysiological examination report submitted?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?

common_queries: CSF report does not suggest elevated protein, or protein levels are within normal range without justification
Electrophysiological evidence does not show demyelination (prolonged F-wave, distal latencies, decreased conduction velocities, or conduction block) without justification
Clinical Notes with evaluation findings, indications, or planned treatment missing or incomplete at pre-authorization
Cerebrospinal Fluid (CSF) analysis or Nerve Conduction study (NCS) report missing or incomplete at pre-authorization
Indoor case papers with treatment details missing or incomplete at claim submission
Electrophysiological examination reports missing or incomplete at claim submission
Discharge summary missing or incomplete at claim submission

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/DNB equivalent in (General Medicine, Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Guillian Barre Syndrome only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation.
GBS is an acute frequently sever fulminant polyradiculoneuropathy, usually presenting as ascending
Plasmapheresis for a total of 4-5 exchanges over 7-10 days.
